content.text: **Document Title:** Internal Developer Portal - Project Provisioning and Management System
**Document Owner:** [AI ASSUMPTION - TO BE CONFIRMED] Technology Organization Leadership / Platform Engineering Team
[USER-PROVIDED] The purpose of this document is to define the requirements for creating an Internal Developer Portal that serves as a single stop for all Engineers to:

items: Create new projects
Add members to existing projects
Remove members from existing projects
Automatically provision required backend scaffolding and infrastructure

content.text: [AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] This portal aims to eliminate manual, repetitive provisioning tasks, reduce time-to-productivity for engineering teams, and enforce organizational standards across all project creation activities.
[USER-PROVIDED] Engineers currently face significant friction when setting up new projects:

items: Engineer submits a request to Tooling team to create a Bitbucket repository
Engineer submits a ticket to Saviynt to get access to the repository
Engineer submits another request to Saviynt to add team members to the repository
Engineer generates a CI/CD pipeline to support the repository
Engineer manually creates boilerplate code as the first "Hello World" code into the repository
Engineer tests all functions manually
Engineers must navigate across multiple systems to complete project setup
Significant manual work required at each step
Context switching between different tools and interfaces
No consistent process across teams for project creation
Inconsistent folder structures, naming conventions, and configurations
Knowledge silos and tribal knowledge dependencies

content.text: [AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] The current state results in:

items: Extended time-to-first-commit for new projects (estimated days vs. minutes)
High ticket volume for Tooling and Access Management teams
Inconsistent project structures leading to maintenance challenges
Poor developer experience and reduced productivity
Risk of security/compliance gaps due to manual provisioning errors

rows: [AI ASSUMPTION] Overall project approval, funding, and strategic alignment
[AI ASSUMPTION] Define engineering standards and adoption requirements
[USER-PROVIDED] Maintain template definitions, approve project requests via ServiceNow, manage Admin Panel, resolve provisioning failures
[USER-PROVIDED] Use portal to create projects, add/remove team members
[AI ASSUMPTION] Build and maintain the portal, integrate with backend systems
[AI ASSUMPTION] Review access provisioning workflows and security controls
[AI ASSUMPTION] Configure approval workflows and integration triggers

items: New Project Setup workflow
Add Member to existing Project workflow
Remove Member from existing Project workflow
Backend scaffolding automation for each scenario
Bitbucket repository creation via API
Saviynt access provisioning for requesters and team members
Basic folder structure creation based on project type and language
ServiceNow work request submission from portal
Tooling team approval process in ServiceNow
Automated provisioning triggered upon ServiceNow approval
Admin Panel for Tooling team to manage template definitions
Template mapping to project type + language combinations
Template versioning and updates (applies to new projects only)
Wizard-based interface asking targeted questions
Smart defaults and narrowed-down options
Asynchronous processing with email notifications
Confirmation emails with project details and next steps
Folder structure varies by project type (microservice, batch job, frontend app, etc.)
Language-specific templates (Java, Python, Node.js, React, etc.)
Template library maintained by Tooling team
SSO authentication for portal access
Role-based access (engineers can create projects, Tooling team accesses Admin Panel)
Audit logging of all provisioning activities
Harness CI/CD pipeline setup (separate process for now)
Boilerplate application code beyond basic folder structure
Ongoing automated team member synchronization
Project deletion or archival workflows
Non-standard project configurations requiring manual intervention
Projects requiring special security controls or compliance sign-off
Custom infrastructure requests beyond standard templates
[USER-PROVIDED] Engineers directed to contact EAToolingTeam@xyz.com for non-standard scenarios
Integration with monitoring/observability tools
Cost tracking or chargeback mechanisms
Project analytics or usage dashboards (beyond basic admin visibility)
Self-service pipeline modification or infrastructure changes

rows: [USER-PROVIDED] Eliminate manual ticket-based provisioning for Bitbucket repository creation
[AI ASSUMPTION] 100% of standard project requests automated; zero tickets to Tooling team for repo creation
[USER-PROVIDED] Automate Saviynt access provisioning for new projects and team member changes
[AI ASSUMPTION] 100% of access requests automated; zero tickets to Saviynt team
[USER-PROVIDED] Establish single entry point for all project provisioning activities across Technology Organization
[AI ASSUMPTION] 100% adoption rate; portal becomes default tool for project creation
[USER-PROVIDED] Enforce standardized folder structures and project configurations
[AI ASSUMPTION] 100% of projects follow template standards; zero configuration drift
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Reduce time-to-first-commit for new projects
Baseline: Days → Target: Minutes (< 5 minutes end-to-end)
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] Improve developer experience and satisfaction
Developer NPS score improvement; reduced onboarding friction

items: **Metric:** Manual tickets eliminated per month
**Baseline:** Estimated 200-400 tickets/month (Bitbucket creation + Saviynt access requests + team member changes)
**Post-Implementation:** < 20 tickets/month (edge cases only)
**Impact:** 90-95% reduction in ticket volume
**Time Savings:** 15-30 minutes per ticket × 180-380 tickets = 45-190 hours/month saved across Tooling and Saviynt teams
**Currency Value:** $50-75/hour loaded cost × 45-190 hours = $2,250-$14,250/month savings
**Annual Savings:** $27,000-$171,000/year
**Metric:** Time to provision new project
**Baseline:** 2-5 days (waiting for tickets, manual setup)
**Post-Implementation:** < 5 minutes (automated)
**Impact:** 99% time reduction
**Affected Users:** Estimated 50-100 new projects/month across Technology Organization
**Productivity Gain:** 2-5 days × 50-100 projects = 100-500 engineer-days/month recovered
**Currency Value:** $500/day loaded cost × 100-500 days = $50,000-$250,000/month
**Metric:** Configuration errors and rework
**Baseline:** Estimated 10-20% of manual setups require rework
**Post-Implementation:** < 2% (template-driven automation)
**Impact:** 80-90% reduction in setup errors
**Time Savings:** 2-4 hours rework × 5-20 incidents/month = 10-80 hours/month
**Annual Value:** $6,000-$48,000/year
**Conservative Estimate:** $633,000/year
**Optimistic Estimate:** $3,219,000/year
**Payback Period:** [AI ASSUMPTION - TO BE CONFIRMED] 3-6 months (assuming development cost of $150,000-$300,000)
Improved security posture through consistent access provisioning
Better compliance audit trail
Reduced onboarding time for new engineers
Increased engineering satisfaction and retention
Foundation for future automation initiatives

rows: [USER-PROVIDED] Portal shall present a wizard-based interface for new project creation
[USER-PROVIDED] Wizard shall ask: "What are you building?" with options for project types (microservice, batch job, frontend app, etc.)
[AI ASSUMPTION] Initial list: Microservice, Batch Job, Frontend App, Full-Stack App, Library, Data Pipeline
[USER-PROVIDED] Wizard shall ask: "What language/framework?" with options varying by project type
[AI ASSUMPTION] Examples: Java/Spring Boot, Python/Flask, Node.js, React, Angular
[USER-PROVIDED] Wizard shall collect project name from user
[AI ASSUMPTION] Validation rules to be defined (naming conventions, uniqueness)
[USER-PROVIDED] Wizard shall allow optional addition of team members with role assignment
[USER-PROVIDED] Wizard shall make smart decisions and provide narrowed-down options based on previous selections
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Wizard shall validate all inputs before submission (required fields, format validation, duplicate checking)
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] Wizard shall display summary/confirmation screen before final submission
[USER-PROVIDED] Portal shall create ServiceNow work request upon wizard submission
[USER-PROVIDED] Portal shall validate request in the tool before submitting to ServiceNow
[USER-PROVIDED] ServiceNow work request shall route to Tooling team for approval
[USER-PROVIDED] Tooling team shall review and approve/reject requests in ServiceNow
[USER-PROVIDED] ServiceNow approval event shall trigger automated provisioning workflow
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] ServiceNow work request shall include all wizard inputs and metadata for approval decision
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] Portal shall provide status tracking for submitted requests (Pending Approval, Approved, Provisioning, Complete, Failed)
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall create Bitbucket repository via API upon ServiceNow approval
[USER-PROVIDED] Repository shall be created with folder structure based on project type and language
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall seed repository with README.md file
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall seed repository with boilerplate code folder based on language/framework
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Repository shall include standard configuration files (.gitignore, build files, etc.) appropriate to language
Examples: pom.xml for Java, package.json for Node.js
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] Repository naming shall follow organizational conventions (validation enforced in wizard)
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall handle Bitbucket API failures gracefully with retry logic
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall provision Saviynt access for project requester upon ServiceNow approval
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall provision Saviynt access for all team members specified in wizard
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] System shall assign appropriate access roles based on selections in wizard (Admin, Developer, Read-Only)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] System shall synchronize access between Bitbucket and Saviynt
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall handle Saviynt API failures gracefully with retry logic
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall provide Admin Panel accessible only to Tooling team
[USER-PROVIDED] Admin Panel shall allow Tooling team to add new templates
[USER-PROVIDED] Admin Panel shall allow Tooling team to update existing templates
[USER-PROVIDED] Admin Panel shall allow mapping templates to project type + language combinations
[USER-PROVIDED] Admin Panel shall allow retiring or versioning templates
[USER-PROVIDED] Template updates shall apply only to new projects going forward (no retroactive changes)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Admin Panel shall provide template preview/validation before publishing
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] Admin Panel shall maintain audit log of all template changes
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall send email notification to requester upon successful provisioning completion
[USER-PROVIDED] Email shall include confirmation that project is ready
[USER-PROVIDED] Email shall include direct link to Bitbucket repository
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Email shall include git clone command for easy copy-paste
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Email shall include summary of what was provisioned (repo, access, team members)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Email shall include next steps or getting started instructions
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall send email notification to requester if provisioning fails
[USER-PROVIDED] Failure email shall indicate that Tooling team has been notified and will resolve backend
[USER-PROVIDED] System shall alert Tooling team of any provisioning failures with sufficient context for troubleshooting
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall send email notification upon ServiceNow approval (before provisioning begins)

sections.title: # FR-007: Add Member to Existing Project

rows: [USER-PROVIDED] Portal shall provide "Add Member to existing Project" workflow from landing page

[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Workflow shall allow user to search/select user to add
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Workflow shall allow selection of role for new member (Admin, Developer, Read-Only)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] System shall provision access in both Bitbucket and Saviynt
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall send notification to requester and new member upon completion

sections.title: # FR-008: Remove Member from Existing Project

rows: [USER-PROVIDED] Portal shall provide "Remove Member from Project" workflow from landing page
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Workflow shall allow user to search/select existing project

[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Workflow shall allow selection of member to remove

[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall send notification to requester upon completion

[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Portal shall be accessible via internal SharePoint link

[USER-PROVIDED] Provisioning workflow shall complete within few minutes (target: < 5 minutes end-to-end)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Portal shall be available 24/7 with 99.5% uptime
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Portal shall support concurrent usage by entire Technology Organization (estimated 500-2000 engineers)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] Portal shall authenticate users via SSO (corporate identity provider)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] All API communications shall use encrypted channels (TLS 1.2+)
[AI ASSUMPTION - HIGH CONFIDENCE] System shall maintain audit log of all provisioning activities for minimum 2 years
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] Portal UI shall be responsive and support modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari)
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] Wizard workflow shall be completable in < 3 minutes for standard projects
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall provide clear error messages and recovery guidance for all failure scenarios
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall retry failed API calls up to 3 times with exponential backoff
[AI ASSUMPTION - MEDIUM CONFIDENCE] System shall gracefully degrade if non-critical services are unavailable
[AI ASSUMPTION - LOW CONFIDENCE] Portal shall comply with organizational data privacy and security policies
[AI ASSUMPTION - LOW CONFIDENCE] System shall support rollback of failed provisioning attempts

[AI ASSUMPTION] I have the right structure for scheduled data processing tasks
[AI ASSUMPTION] Check the status of my project creation request
[AI ASSUMPTION] I know if it's pending approval, provisioning, or complete

[AI ASSUMPTION] I have a clear escalation path for special requirements

[AI ASSUMPTION] New engineers can be productive quickly without extensive hand-holding

[AI ASSUMPTION - LOW CONFIDENCE] Portal must demonstrate ROI within 6-12 months of launch
